# Power, Cooling, and Rack Density

Inference clusters are constrained by heat and power before they are constrained
by architectural taste.

## Chapter Job

Explain how power delivery and cooling choices shape AI hardware density,
availability, and operating cost.

## Cooling Choices

| Cooling model | Why teams choose it | Tradeoff to inspect |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Air cooling | Simpler operations and broad facility compatibility | Lower practical rack density |
| Rear-door heat exchanger | Transitional path for denser racks | Integration and airflow discipline |
| Direct-to-chip liquid cooling | Higher density and thermal headroom | Facility readiness and maintenance |
| Immersion cooling | High-density thermal envelope | Ecosystem maturity and service model |

## Inference-Specific Questions

- Does the workload create steady load or bursty load?
- Can the facility support sustained accelerator utilization?
- What happens when thermal throttling starts?
- Are power caps configured intentionally or discovered during incidents?
- Can the same rack support both prefill-heavy and decode-heavy workloads?

## Artifact

Create a rack design card:

- Rack density target.
- Accelerator count.
- Power envelope.
- Cooling method.
- Network ports per node.
- Failure domain.
- Measured utilization assumption.
